derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frames ARE the proof moment)
proof_clarity.note: Proof-clarity rewards single-shot legible payoffs (build+test hybrids, body before/after anchors, numeric counter freeze + named artifact) and penalizes observation/identity endpoints with no artifact, cognitive/head-framed payoffs, and stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. Added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector and final re-rank both reward mechanism visibility at the concrete premise level. No hand-picked top-5 — every weight reads a factual field on the motif atom and cites a corpus indicator.

drivers.source: body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in motif copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES
title premise line signals a single-frame payoff (same-shot / weigh-in / freeze-frame / break point / call-it moment) — wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot end-over-start reveal)
first_frame_action names an action verb + at least one of {gauge, physical object} — the challenge is legible at t=0s; HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause applied at the opening

visual_action_short names a state→state change (cut between, day1 vs dayN, growing stack/pile/tower, slides out, pedals turning, mile-counter) — wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (legible progress / before-after)

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frame at t=0s IS comprehension)
visual_legibility.note: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the motif atom along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No rejected category layer — the six axes are factual fields on the motif and the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

top_indicators.effect_size: Face 0.31 higher at drops, Text 0.33 higher at drops
top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.
top_indicators.why: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.

top_indicators.quantification: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.
top_indicators.rule: Put your weakest/setup content in the first 20%. Never let the worst moment happen after 30%.
top_indicators.why: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the motif atom on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no rejected category layer; no hand-picked top 5.

state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (I Built A Bike Out Of 2x4s And Rode It 5 Miles To See What Broke First — The Build Held…) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 9 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.78 from 5 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.6 from 5 drivers (+5/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

evidence_summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

ideas.title: I Hand-Wrote 300 Letters — One To Every Contact In My Phone — The Timer Hit 300 Exactly
ideas.one_line_premise: I hand-write letters — one for every contact in my phone at a desk beside a stack of stamped envelopes, narrating every sensation in my hand until the timer hits exactly 300.

concept.title: I Hand-Wrote 300 Letters — One To Every Contact In My Phone — The Timer Hit 300 Exactly
concept.logline: I hand-write letters — one for every contact in my phone at a desk beside a stack of stamped envelopes, narrating every sensation in my hand until the timer hits exactly 300.
concept.promise: You're watching me hand-write letters — one for every contact in my phone — the question is where the counter lands on a specific number.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the timer hits exactly 300; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies a round target (300); payoff lands on a specific, non-round value (300) — specificity over-delivers vs the rounded promise.

opening.first_frame: Close on mid-line of handwriting with a finished stack in frame. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — 300, and my hand is going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on a hand writing with a stack of addressed envelopes growing for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter a desk beside a stack of stamped envelopes; a hand writing with a stack of addressed envelopes growing; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first hand sensation narration ("my hand is bigger"); counter begins to tick
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating a hand writing with a stack of addressed envelopes growing with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the timer hits exactly 300; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the timer hits exactly 300; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with hand + impact word: "hand's numb — insane."

first_5s: mid-line of handwriting with a finished stack in frame
hook_quarter: a hand writing with a stack of addressed envelopes growing
mid: alternate hand close-ups with a hand writing with a stack of addressed envelopes growing
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

visual_legibility.note: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the motif atom along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No rejected category layer — the six axes are factual fields on the motif and the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

top_indicators.rule: NEVER name materials. Show the result, not the process. 'Watch what happens' > 'I used carbon fiber reinforced with graphene layers'.
top_indicators.why: Word impact scores: plastic(-0.171), solid(-0.163), fiber(-0.138), materials(-0.136), carbon(-0.109), built(-0.095), metal(-0.095). Drop-exclusive words: 'plate', 'chest', 'magnets', 'laser'. These ar

top_indicators.key: risk:face + text without action (talking head trap)
top_indicators.effect_size: Face 0.31 higher at drops, Text 0.33 higher at drops
top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.
top_indicators.why: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.

top_indicators.quantification: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.
top_indicators.rule: Put your weakest/setup content in the first 20%. Never let the worst moment happen after 30%.
top_indicators.why: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

top_indicators.why: highest-value single title-word pattern in corpus

creator_fit.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass and MMR fill both reward maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)

single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the motif atom on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no rejected category layer; no hand-picked top 5.

state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

why_it_works: Specific premise (I Hand-Wrote 300 Letters — One To Every Contact In My Phone — The Timer Hit 300 Exactly) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.

Vocabulary: commits to 8 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Visual-legibility: score=0.48 from 5 drivers (+5/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.

ideas.title: I Ran The Same Mile Every Day For 365 Days — The First And Last Run Are The Same Shot
ideas.one_line_premise: I run the same neighborhood mile every morning and film the same starting frame on day 1 and day N on the exact same one-mile neighborhood loop, narrating every sensation in my foot until the final-365 days frame holds the whole transformation in one shot.

concept.title: I Ran The Same Mile Every Day For 365 Days — The First And Last Run Are The Same Shot
concept.logline: I run the same neighborhood mile every morning and film the same starting frame on day 1 and day N on the exact same one-mile neighborhood loop, narrating every sensation in my foot until the final-365 days frame holds the whole transformation in one shot.
concept.promise: You're watching me run the same neighborhood mile every morning and film the same starting frame on day 1 and day N — the question is where the before/after lands in one shot.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the final-365 days frame holds the whole transformation in one shot; a single word of reaction lands as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies a legible answer to the premise (365 days); payoff delivers the reveal as one frame/phrase — specificity of the single shot over-delivers vs the rounded premise.

opening.first_frame: Close on the exact same driveway starting frame with a big day-counter label in the corner. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — 365 days, and my foot is going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter the exact same one-mile neighborhood loop; a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first foot sensation narration ("my foot is bigger"); counter begins to tick
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the final-365 days frame holds the whole transformation in one shot; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the final-365 days frame holds the whole transformation in one shot; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with foot + impact word: "foot's numb — insane."

first_5s: the exact same driveway starting frame with a big day-counter label in the corner
hook_quarter: a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N
mid: alternate foot close-ups with a cut between two runs at the same starting line on day 1 and day N
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

It reads the motif atom along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

top_indicators.quantification: Title contains making/build/creat/construct — 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M

top_indicators.why: highest-value single title-word pattern in corpus

drivers_triggered.source: inverse of visual_is_workshop (r=+0.236); HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause favors tactile fixed framing over roaming outdoor
top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
title_making_keyword ($24M avg for build/test format) + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-shot before/after or build-until-fail visible payoff)

creator_fit.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass and MMR fill both reward maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
top_indicators.why: the proof MOMENT is an action frame — visible build/test/body/counter state in one shot
single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.filter: factual fields on the motif atom × endpoint kind — each driver exposes the matched verb/anchor/artifact and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: diversity bucket=body_transformation AND motif copy names a before/after anchor — wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (single-frame before/after payoff structure)
wave11_12.end_begin_ratio — qualitative reveal is single-shot legible when motif copy names a concrete artifact (scored in D)
first_frame_action / visual_action_short names a physical artifact or overlay — HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause (artifact visible in the proof shot)
diversity bucket=body_transformation|build_test AND action_intensity ≥ medium — PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE + HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES stack (daily act itself is visible action)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the motif atom on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no rejected category layer; no hand-picked top 5.

top_indicators.quantification: Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops
top_indicators.why: frame-1 comprehensibility IS an action frame at t=0s — action verb in first_frame_action is the opening-second proxy for this cause
visible body_part_phrase (foot/shoulders/stomach/hand) has a surface to film; feeling/head/mind/brain/memory do not
title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (I Ran The Same Mile Every Day For 365 Days — The First And Last Run Are The Same Shot) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 9 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.66 from 4 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.5 from 4 drivers (+4/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

evidence_summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

ideas.title: I Carried A 100-Lb Sandbag For 10 Miles — The Mile Counter Froze At 10.3 miles
ideas.one_line_premise: I carry a 100-lb sandbag on my shoulder on a long stretch of road, narrating every sensation in my foot until the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles.

concept.title: I Carried A 100-Lb Sandbag For 10 Miles — The Mile Counter Froze At 10.3 miles
concept.logline: I carry a 100-lb sandbag on my shoulder on a long stretch of road, narrating every sensation in my foot until the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles.
concept.promise: You're watching me carry a 100-lb sandbag on my shoulder — the question is where the counter lands on a specific number.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles; a single sensation word appears as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies a round target (10 miles); payoff lands on a specific, non-round value (10.3 miles) — specificity over-delivers vs the rounded promise.

opening.first_frame: Close on sandbag heaved onto my shoulder mid-step. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — 10 miles, and my foot is going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on the bag carried step by step with a distance overlay for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter a long stretch of road; the bag carried step by step with a distance overlay; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first foot sensation narration ("my foot is bigger"); counter begins to tick
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating the bag carried step by step with a distance overlay with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the mile counter freezes at 10.3 miles; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with foot + impact word: "foot's numb — insane."

first_5s: sandbag heaved onto my shoulder mid-step
hook_quarter: the bag carried step by step with a distance overlay
mid: alternate foot close-ups with the bag carried step by step with a distance overlay
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

drivers.source: inverse of visual_is_workshop (r=+0.236); HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause favors tactile fixed framing over roaming outdoor
top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (numeric counter freeze = single-shot visible payoff)

creator_fit.note: Creator-fit is added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector rewards maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level. Weights derived from indicator strengths above — no hand-curated creator taste beyond what the corpus already implies.

drivers.source: top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + wave11_12.end_begin_ratio — numeric counter freeze is single-shot legible proof
first_frame_action / visual_action_short names a physical artifact or overlay — HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause (artifact visible in the proof shot)

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frames ARE the proof moment)
proof_clarity.note: Proof-clarity rewards single-shot legible payoffs (build+test hybrids, body before/after anchors, numeric counter freeze + named artifact) and penalizes observation/identity endpoints with no artifact, cognitive/head-framed payoffs, and stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. Added to the combo score so the diversity-aware selector and final re-rank both reward mechanism visibility at the concrete premise level. No hand-picked top-5 — every weight reads a factual field on the motif atom and cites a corpus indicator.

drivers.source: body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in motif copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES

endpoint is numeric/body/build_test AND motif has no builtin reveal — composeTitle() appends a single-frame freeze ("The Counter Froze At N" / "The Timer Hit N Exactly" / "The Mile Counter Froze At N" / "My {part} Quit First" / "The Build Held Until N"); wave11_12.end_begin_ratio
first_frame_action names an action verb + at least one of {gauge, physical object} — the challenge is legible at t=0s; HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause applied at the opening

visual_action_short names a state→state change (cut between, day1 vs dayN, growing stack/pile/tower, slides out, pedals turning, mile-counter) — wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (legible progress / before-after)

derived_from_indicators: retention-patterns.top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops — action frame at t=0s IS comprehension)
visual_legibility.note: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the motif atom along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. No rejected category layer — the six axes are factual fields on the motif and the title string. No hand-picked top 5.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

build_phases.rationale: Build phases are validated against the top-5 retention predictors + narrative_arc_analysis (best/worst arc labels) + wave7 retention-shape evidence.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

build_phases.filter: retention-curve predictors ranked by |r_with_views| across the video pool

best_arc.description: 54% of all videos follow this arc. Consistently building above_baseline from hook to end.

worst_arc.description: Front-loaded content that loses viewers progressively. 10x worse than steady_rise.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the motif atom on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no rejected category layer; no hand-picked top 5.

why_it_works: Specific premise (I Carried A 100-Lb Sandbag For 10 Miles — The Mile Counter Froze At 10.3 miles) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 9 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=0.34 from 2 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=0.56 from 5 drivers (+5/-0) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

ideas.title: I Tried To Learn 500 Words Of A New Language In One Day — A Native Speaker Tested Me At The End
ideas.one_line_premise: I drill flashcards in a language I don’t speak from morning until night, then sit with a native speaker who quizzes every word at a kitchen table with a native speaker sitting opposite me at dusk, narrating every sensation in my feeling until the observation from the last 500 lands as a single on-screen sentence.

concept.title: I Tried To Learn 500 Words Of A New Language In One Day — A Native Speaker Tested Me At The End
concept.logline: I drill flashcards in a language I don’t speak from morning until night, then sit with a native speaker who quizzes every word at a kitchen table with a native speaker sitting opposite me at dusk, narrating every sensation in my feeling until the observation from the last 500 lands as a single on-screen sentence.
concept.promise: You're watching me drill flashcards in a language I don’t speak from morning until night, then sit with a native speaker who quizzes every word — the question is where one observation replaces every assumption.
concept.payoff: Final 5% of runtime: the observation from the last 500 lands as a single on-screen sentence; a single word of reaction lands as overlay.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies a legible answer to the premise (500); payoff delivers the reveal as one frame/phrase — specificity of the single shot over-delivers vs the rounded premise.

opening.first_frame: Close on a stack of flashcards with a single unfamiliar word on the top card and a count overlay at zero. Counter visible. No explanatory overlay; composition emphasizes direct address (rho=0.14 vs log_views).
opening.first_line: Go — 500, and my feeling is going to tell you when it's over.
opening.opening_action: Camera locked on flashcards moving from an unknown-pile to a known-pile with a running tally for the first 3 seconds; I enter motion inside the first second.

build_phases.beat: enter a kitchen table with a native speaker sitting opposite me at dusk; flashcards moving from an unknown-pile to a known-pile with a running tally; premise named on screen
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%. Design rule v3 #6 (5.4x gap).

build_phases.beat: first feeling sensation narration ("my feeling is bigger"); counter begins to tick
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at ~22%. Sensory-word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: escalating flashcards moving from an unknown-pile to a known-pile with a running tally with sensory updates; utterances < 10 words; no pauses > 1s
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in distress / counter. Peak-word density increases.

build_phases.beat: peak zone — slow to 3.0 w/s; short sentence on a single sensation; reaction → wide on the count or distance milestone
build_phases.note: Peak 60-80%. Peak-speaking-rate 3.86 wps confirmed; utterance 7.9 words.

build_phases.beat: the observation from the last 500 lands as a single on-screen sentence; single-word overlay (sensation); wide shot of the final state
build_phases.note: Golden final 5% + END_RECOVERY. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At 95% of runtime, the observation from the last 500 lands as a single on-screen sentence; camera holds on the final position; I say one word and the overlay freezes.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with feeling + impact word: "feeling's numb — insane."

first_5s: a stack of flashcards with a single unfamiliar word on the top card and a count overlay at zero
hook_quarter: flashcards moving from an unknown-pile to a known-pile with a running tally
mid: alternate feeling close-ups with flashcards moving from an unknown-pile to a known-pile with a running tally
text overlay at beat moments only

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

It reads the motif atom along six axes (invisible body part, cognitive verb without physical action, explicit cognitive surface, title-payoff phrasing, frame-1 comprehensibility, and visual state-contrast) plus an identity/mystery frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that pass proof-clarity via cosmetic artifact tokens ("stack", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal is a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Structure[nadir_before_climax]: best_after_worst 3.3M vs best_before_worst 650K (5x).
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.
Pre-upload lever[hook_word_ratio]: r=-0.2693, lower → more views.
Pre-upload lever[visual_variety_entropy]: r=0.2692, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_word_count]: r=0.2639, higher → more views.
Pre-upload lever[transcript_char_count]: r=0.2557, higher → more views.

build_phases.rationale: Build phases are validated against the top-5 retention predictors + narrative_arc_analysis (best/worst arc labels) + wave7 retention-shape evidence.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

top_indicators.rule: NEVER name materials. Show the result, not the process. 'Watch what happens' > 'I used carbon fiber reinforced with graphene layers'.
top_indicators.why: Word impact scores: plastic(-0.171), solid(-0.163), fiber(-0.138), materials(-0.136), carbon(-0.109), built(-0.095), metal(-0.095). Drop-exclusive words: 'plate', 'chest', 'magnets', 'laser'. These ar

top_indicators.key: risk:face + text without action (talking head trap)
top_indicators.effect_size: Face 0.31 higher at drops, Text 0.33 higher at drops
top_indicators.quantification: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.
top_indicators.rule: Never have Tyler just talking to camera with text overlays. If his face is on screen, he must be DOING something physical.
top_indicators.why: GPT-4o-mini vision: face more visible at drops (3.51) than peaks (3.20). Text more present at drops (3.73 vs 3.40). Face alone doesn't retain — face + explaining + text = drop. Face + action = peak.

top_indicators.quantification: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.
top_indicators.rule: Never put boring content after 60%. Any setup/explanation must happen in the first 30%. The back half must be pure payoff.
top_indicators.why: Viewer fatigue r=-0.31: later drops 2.15x steeper. Only 3/472 late drops were recovered. Once viewers start leaving in the back half, they don't come back.

top_indicators.quantification: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.
top_indicators.rule: Put your weakest/setup content in the first 20%. Never let the worst moment happen after 30%.
top_indicators.why: Top quartile videos have worst moment at 17%. Bottom quartile at 49%. Successful videos front-load their worst content where it's recoverable.

top_indicators.why: phrases like "link in bio" / "see what happens" / "on my profile" are enriched at drops

top_indicators.rule: avoid stakes-based framing ("if this fails…") — use transformation or mystery
verbal energy drives retention more than visual. Pair visual beats with spoken sensory language.

creator_fit.rationale: Creator-fit is derived purely from corpus indicators that empirically prefer maker/body/workshop DNA: title-level making/build keywords (pat_making_v2, title_making_keyword), workshop/hands-visible framing (visual_is_workshop, pre_workshop_x_making, tension_x_workshop), sensory-over-technical language (top peak/drop causes), and feasible single-shot visible-proof endpoints (HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES, end_begin_ratio). Every weight in computeCreatorFit() cites a specific indicator; no hand-curated creator taste is added beyond what these indicators already imply.

top_indicators.quantification: Title contains making/build/creat/construct — 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M

top_indicators.why: highest-value single title-word pattern in corpus

top_indicators.quantification: Video shot in workshop-like environment (42/203 positive, 2.6x)
Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops

top_indicators.why: single-shot visible payoff structure rewards transformation/build endings

creator_fit.filter: indicators with published r/r_partial/delta_r2 in the evidence lattice above a minimum effect threshold

drivers_triggered.source: indicator_registry.visual_is_workshop r_direct=+0.236 (42/203 pos, 2.6x); pre_workshop_x_making r_partial=+0.229; tension_x_workshop r_partial=+0.283
top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (sensory-rate regression weight +1.59; per-word positive deltas +0.04-0.06 above-baseline in word-retention-impact.json)
tactile frame preserved without triggering TECHNICAL_MATERIAL_LANGUAGE drop cause — sensory > technical axis

creator_fit.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass and MMR fill both reward maker/body/workshop alignment at the concrete premise level.

proof_clarity.rationale: Proof-clarity rewards ideas with a single-shot legible payoff: build+test hybrids (verb combo match), body-transformation with an explicit before/after anchor (same shot / weigh-in / day 1 vs day N), numeric endpoints with a named artifact in frame (counter/overlay/stack/tower/hull/pedals). Penalizes abstract observation/identity payoffs without artifacts, head/feeling-framed low-action payoffs, and repetition_outreach stacks whose contents are untestable to the viewer. The signal is factual — it reads the title premise line, logline_action, first_frame_action, visual_action_short, endpoint.kind, action_intensity, and body_part_phrase — not taste.

top_indicators.quantification: Action-frame share at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
top_indicators.why: the proof MOMENT is an action frame — visible build/test/body/counter state in one shot
single-shot before/after / build-test / counter-freeze payoff structure
body/physical payoff reads on camera; cognitive payoff does not
object interaction makes the proof legible vs face-only reaction

proof_clarity.filter: factual fields on the motif atom × endpoint kind — each driver exposes the matched verb/anchor/artifact and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: first_frame_action / visual_action_short names a physical artifact or overlay — HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause (artifact visible in the proof shot)

body_part_phrase ∈ {head, feeling, mind, brain, memory} — inverse of HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (cognitive change has no legible body surface regardless of action intensity)
HANDS_VISIBLE match in visual_action_short / first_frame_action — indicator_registry.visual_is_workshop axis (hands-on-object framing)

proof_clarity.selection_effect: Added to the combo score so the diversity pass, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all reward single-shot visible proof at the concrete premise level.

visual_legibility.rationale: Visual-legibility is endpoint-independent. It reads the motif atom on six axes — (1) invisible body_part_phrase, (2) cognitive verb without a physical-action verb, (3) explicit cognitive-surface copy, (4) title premise line reveal phrasing classified physical-reveal vs verbal/observational, (5) frame-1 comprehensibility (action verb + gauge/object in first_frame_action), (6) state-contrast in visual_action_short — plus a mystery/identity frame-signal check. Designed to catch motifs that gamed proof-clarity via cosmetic proof tokens ("stack of flashcards", "tally", "count overlay") while the actual reveal was a verbal quiz, a social observation, or a cognitive verdict. Every driver exposes the matched phrase or stem and cites an on-disk corpus indicator; no rejected category layer; no hand-picked top 5.

top_indicators.quantification: Action frames appear 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops
top_indicators.why: frame-1 comprehensibility IS an action frame at t=0s — action verb in first_frame_action is the opening-second proxy for this cause
visible body_part_phrase (foot/shoulders/stomach/hand) has a surface to film; feeling/head/mind/brain/memory do not
title premise line reveal phrasing classified: physical single-shot reveals align, verbal/observational reveals regress
state-contrast in visual_action_short (cut between, growing stack, slides out, pedals turning) is the hands-on-object axis applied across the build

top_indicators.quantification: Title contains making/build/creat/construct — 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M
top_indicators.why: title_making_keyword pattern aligns with physical reveal phrasing

visual_legibility.filter: six factual axes on the motif atom + title string — each driver exposes the matched stem/phrase/body-part and the corpus indicator it cites

drivers_triggered.source: body_part_phrase ∈ {feeling, head, mind, brain, memory} — no visible body surface for the payoff to land on; inverse of PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE (top peak cause, +1.59 weight) and HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES (28% at peaks vs 8% at drops)
body_parts lists at least one visible limb/torso — cuts to per-beat close-ups legible; top_3_peak_causes.PHYSICAL_SENSORY_LANGUAGE
physical-action verb stem present in motif copy — the daily act is filmable action; top_3_peak_causes.HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES
title premise line signals a verbal / observational / cognitive reveal — wave11_12.end_begin_ratio requires a single-frame visible payoff, not a verdict the viewer has to trust
first_frame_action names an action verb + at least one of {gauge, physical object} — the challenge is legible at t=0s; HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES peak cause applied at the opening

visual_action_short names a state→state change (cut between, day1 vs dayN, growing stack/pile/tower, slides out, pedals turning, mile-counter) — wave11_12.end_begin_ratio (legible progress / before-after)

visual_legibility.selection_effect: Added to the combo score alongside proof_clarity so the diversity-aware selector, MMR fill, AND the final blueprint re-rank all push cognitive/abstract/verbal-reveal motifs below zero before any top-5 slot is assigned.

scorecard_targets.rationale: Scorecard targets are validated against the per-dimension distribution stats (mean, p75, p90) computed across the video scorecard corpus.

top_indicators.quantification: Per-video 0-10 score on over_delivery dimension across n=50 scorecards
Per-video 0-10 score on late_retention dimension across n=50 scorecards
Per-video 0-10 score on consistency dimension across n=50 scorecards
Per-video 0-10 score on smoothness dimension across n=50 scorecards
Per-video 0-10 score on sensory_language dimension across n=50 scorecards
Per-video 0-10 score on material_avoidance dimension across n=50 scorecards
Per-video 0-10 score on early_momentum dimension across n=50 scorecards

scorecard_targets.filter: per-dimension percentile stats across n=50 scorecards

hook_retention_20s.rationale: Additive model: corpus mean retention@20s (79.3%) + design deltas keyed to top peak/drop causes and opening density.

top_indicators.why: corpus mean = 79.3%; r_with_views ≈ 0.60 (single strongest view predictor)

top_indicators.quantification: Action frames at best vs worst moments (28% vs 8%)
Avg above-baseline when material words appear

share_propensity.rationale: Additive model: nominal 3% share baseline + peak-phrase triggers, shareable hook taxonomy, golden final 5% payoff, and penalty for CTA-drift phrases.

top_indicators.quantification: Phrase enrichment at above-baseline peaks vs corpus
Phrase enrichment at above-baseline peaks vs corpus
Phrase enrichment at above-baseline peaks vs corpus
Phrase enrichment at drops vs corpus
Phrase enrichment at drops vs corpus
Phrase enrichment at drops vs corpus

keep_rate.rationale: Additive model: corpus mean keep + deltas from over-delivery structure, monotonic rise, and nadir placement — each grounded in an explicit retention-pattern signal.

top_indicators.quantification: median views when nadir precedes climax

view_band.rationale: Design score is mapped onto the video-scorecard total-score quintiles (views_median, p25, p75) and then expanded by the full-model prediction-range multiplier to express residual uncertainty.

top_indicators.quantification: Multiplier used to widen quintile band for uncertainty

view_band.filter: scorecard total-score quintiles (view_median, p25, p75) × full-model range multiplier
view_band.note: MODELED — not a prediction. 72.9% of view variance is external (algorithm, timing, audience).

summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.

why_it_works: Specific premise (I Tried To Learn 500 Words Of A New Language In One Day — A Native Speaker Tested Me At…) — every concrete choice (object, timer, endpoint) survives the validation filter below.
Arc = steady_rise with nadir placed at ~17% (best_after_worst 5x gap).
Hook-retention@20s modeled high (86.8%) — first-20s is the single strongest view predictor (r=0.6).
Over-delivery structure: hook promises less than the 95% payoff delivers (hook_payoff_gap rewards over-delivery, r=-0.52).
Vocabulary: commits to 8 peak words and avoids 6 material-class words.

Proof-clarity: score=-0.06 from 3 drivers (build+test hybrid, body before/after anchor, numeric counter + named artifact) — single-shot legible payoff backed by HIGH_ENERGY_ACTION_FRAMES + end_begin_ratio.

Visual-legibility: score=-0.2 from 6 drivers (+4/-2) — frame-1 comprehensibility + decisive physical reveal in title + state-contrast in the cut-to; penalizes invisible body parts, cognitive verbs, and verbal/observational reveals regardless of endpoint kind.
Validation trace: 19 blueprint sections and 5 modeled metrics carry explicit indicator lineage (pool size, filter, top indicators w/ r/rho/csw and quantification).

evidence_summary.note: Raw totals sum across traces and can double-count indicators reused in multiple sections. "unique_indicator_keys_used" deduplicates.
